structured_answers.answer_text: An IP address is a unique numerical identifier assigned to each device on a network.
Latency is the time delay between sending and receiving data.
CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.
HTTP stands for Hyper Text Transfer Protocol.

Ram is volatile, ROM is non volatile.
RAM:
It is used for temporary storage.
ROM:
It is used for permanent instructions.
A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing traffic. It is used to protect systems from unauthorized access.
The Domain Name System (DNS) is used to translate human readable domain names to IP addresses. This helps computers locate servers.

Load Balancing is the process of distributing incoming network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server is overloaded.
This improves system performance by sharing workload, ensures high availability and scalability.

The OSI (Open System Interconnection) model is a conceptual framework used to understand how data is transmitted over a network.
Physical layer → Transmits raw bits
Data link layer → Handles error detection
Network Layer - Responsible for routing
Transport Layer → Ensures reliable data transfer.
Session Layer - Manages communication session
Data Transmission over the internet follows a structured process using networking protocols and devices.

User Request → The user enters URL in browser. DNS translates it into an IP address.
Data Packet Formation → The data is divided into smaller units called packets.
Protocol Handling
Reassembling
Display.
Key Component :
Sender and Receiver
→ DNS
→ Router & Switcher
- Transmission Media
→ Protocols :
The internet in a combination of Packet switching, routing and Protocol to ensure best, reliable and accurate communication between devices world wide.
